title: True Few-Shot Learning with Language Models

structured.summary: The paper reviews the performance of pretrained language models (LMs) for true few-shot learning, where no held-out examples are used for tuning. It questions whether true few-shot selection criteria like cross-validation and minimum description length can effectively choose model prompts and hyperparameters, finding them to underperform compared to models tuned with held-out examples.

strengths.point: Critical examination of existing methodologies for few-shot learning.
strengths.evidence: The paper contrasts true few-shot learning with methods reliant on held-out examples, suggesting that previous evaluations might have overestimated such models' performance.

strengths.point: Comprehensive evaluation across multiple LMs and tasks.
strengths.evidence: The study evaluates 9 LMs on 3 classification tasks and 41 tasks from the LAMA benchmark, covering a wide range of model sizes and application scenarios.

weaknesses.evidence: The paper applies existing model selection techniques like cross-validation and minimum description length without significant new adaptations for the unique challenges of true few-shot learning.
Results focus on the deficits of current practices without exploring contexts or scenarios where true few-shot learning could be successful.

weaknesses.point: Lack of clarity in defining few-shot learning settings.
weaknesses.evidence: The paper spends a considerable portion of the introduction on differentiating few-shot learning types without clear, succinct definitions, potentially leading to confusion.

questions.question: How can model selection methods be adapted to better suit true few-shot learning?
questions.motivation: Identifying new mechanisms or improvements could advance methodologies tailored to the strict constraints of true few-shot learning.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of novelty in methodology.. The paper applies existing model selection techniques like cross-validation and minimum description length without significant new adaptations for the unique challenges of true few-shot learning
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient exploration of real-world applicability.. Results focus on the deficits of current practices without exploring contexts or scenarios where true few-shot learning could be successful
critique_points.point_003: Lack of clarity in defining few-shot learning settings.. The paper spends a considerable portion of the introduction on differentiating few-shot learning types without clear, succinct definitions, potentially leading to confusion
